steps.text: Start with $b - 72 = \\dfrac{x}{y}$. To solve for $x$, multiply both sides by $y$: $y(b - 72) = x$.
Expand the left side: $y \cdot b - 72 \cdot y = by - 72y$. So $x = by - 72y$.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $x = \\dfrac{b-72}{y}$ — divides instead of multiplies by $y$. Eliminated.
  • B: $x = by - 72$ — multiplied $b$ by $y$ but not 72. Eliminated.
  • C: $x = \\dfrac{by - 72}{y}$ — incorrectly divides the entire expression by $y$ again. Eliminated.
  • D: $x = by - 72y$ — correctly distributes $y$ across both terms. That confirms D is correct.
